title: Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis
chief_complaint: I've been dropping things and my speech has become slurred over the past 6 months. My muscles twitch all over. I've lost weight and I feel weak. My thinking is fine - I know exactly what's happening to me.

physical_exam.Neurologic: Dysarthria (slurred speech). Tongue fasciculations and atrophy. Upper extremity: Fasciculations in biceps, triceps, and intrinsic hand muscles. Weakness (4/5) in hands and arms. Hyperreflexia in all limbs. Positive Hoffman sign. Bilateral Babinski sign. Lower extremity: Spasticity. No sensory deficits.
physical_exam.Cranial: Normal extraocular movements. No ptosis. Facial weakness present.

hidden_data.emg: Active denervation (fibrillations, positive sharp waves) and chronic denervation (large motor units, reduced recruitment) in bulbar, cervical, thoracic, and lumbosacral myotomes. Fulfills Awaji criteria for ALS.
hidden_data.mri_brain: No structural lesion. No cervical stenosis. No demyelinating disease.
hidden_data.anti_gm1: Negative (rules out multifocal motor neuropathy - an ALS mimic)
hidden_data.lumbar_puncture: Normal CSF. No oligoclonal bands. No elevated protein.

correct_diagnosis: Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS) - Definite by Awaji Criteria, Bulbar-Onset
key_learning_points: ALS = progressive degeneration of upper AND lower motor neurons. UMN: Spasticity, hyperreflexia, Babinski. LMN: Weakness, atrophy, fasciculations, cramps
Awaji criteria for diagnosis. El Escorial: Definite (3 regions), Probable (2 regions), Possible (1 region). EMG is essential for diagnosis
Bulbar onset (25%): Dysarthria, dysphagia, tongue fasciculations. Worse prognosis. Limb onset (75%): Hand weakness, foot drop
ALWAYS rule out ALS mimics: Multifocal motor neuropathy (treatable with IVIG), CIDP, cervical myelopathy, syringomyelia, Kennedy disease, lead toxicity

Treatment: Riluzole 50mg BID (modestly slows progression). Edaravone IV (slows decline). NIPPV for respiratory support. PEG tube for nutrition. Multidisciplinary ALS clinic. Median survival 3-5 years from diagnosis
